title: Magnetic Reed Switch for Contact/Position Detection
content: A reed switch is a pair of ferromagnetic contacts hermetically sealed in a glass tube. A permanent magnet within ~10–20 mm closes (N/O) or opens (N/C) the contacts. No power consumption when static; rated for 10⁷–10⁸ operations; dry contact output connects directly to MCU GPIO with a pull-up resistor.
extended_content: Reed switches are commonly used for door/window open-closed detection, limit switches, and liquid level detection. Operating distance (AT — Ampere Turns): typically 15–40 AT to actuate; matched magnets supplied with sensors. Bounce time: 0.5–2 ms — software debounce (20–50 ms window) or RC filter prevents false triggers. Wiring: one terminal to GPIO, other to GND; enable internal pull-up (or add 10 kΩ external). Normally-open (N/O) is most common — circuit closes when magnet approaches. Contact resistance < 0.1 Ω closed; 10⁸ Ω open. Maximum ratings: 1 A, 100 V DC (small through-hole types). For garage-door or window applications, use surface-mount form-factor pairs (sensor + magnet) with 5–15 mm operating gap.
source: Standex-Meder Electronics Reed Switch Fundamentals Application Note; Littelfuse Reed Switch Design Guide
